sections.paragraph:
Ch. to fear, i.q. Hebr. זָחַל prop. to creep along, to go with a quiet gait, like timid persons, furchtfam heranfchleichen. To this corresponds the Syr. ܕܚܶܠ to fear. Arab. دحل to flee, to withdraw, pr. fich davonfchleichen, to withdraw oneself secretly. Constr. followed by מִן קֳדָם (compare יָרֵא מִפְּנֵי), Dan 5:19. Part. דְּחִיל terrible, Dan 2:31 7:7 .

Pael דַּחֵל to make afraid, terrify. Dan 4:2.
